notes: BW3 Noble Victories, Black & White era. Pack = 10 cards: 5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare-or-better slot. Box = 36 packs (no ETB existed for this 2011 set; sealed product was the booster box + theme decks). Set-SPECIFIC rates from Flipside Gaming "Rarity in the Pokemon TCG Part Two", which names Noble Victories explicitly: Ultra Rare (Full Art) ~1:18 packs (=2 per box), Secret Rare ~1:72 packs (=0.5 per box, i.e. ~1 every 2 boxes; NV has exactly 1 secret rare, Pikachu 101/101). Rare slot composition: UR=1/18=0.0556, SR=1/72=0.0139, remainder 0.9305 split between non-holo rare and rare-holo using the BW-era ~1-in-3 holo-rare cadence (~13 holos visible across 36 packs) -> rare-holo 0.3105, rare 0.62. Reverse-holo slot has no dedicated bucket in this set's rarity list, so it is mapped across common/uncommon/rare-holo (a reverse can be any common/uncommon/rare; weighted toward common/uncommon which dominate the pool). confidence=low: era/set-level published ratios from a single curated source, no large per-set pull-rate study available for a 2011 vintage set. Vintage sealed box carries a large collector premium, so booster-EV will sit well below box price — expected, not a rate problem.
